title: Sudden Water Level Surge at Vandri Lake Waterfall Traps Tourists in Palghar - Pratidin Time
full_text:
A sudden surge in water flow at Vandri Lake Waterfall in Maharashtra’s Palghar district left several tourists stranded on Tuesday, turning a routine outing into a life-threatening situation. The incident unfolded during ongoing monsoon rains, as rising currents caught visitors off guard and triggered panic among families at the site.
The incident, captured on video and widely circulated online, has once again raised concerns over tourist safety at waterfalls during the monsoon season.
Visuals from the scene show tourists enjoying the waterfall despite rainfall, before conditions changed abruptly within minutes. Muddy water gushed through the area, rapidly increasing the force of the current and leaving visitors struggling to maintain their footing.
Men, women, and children can be seen holding onto each other as they attempt to move to safer ground, with several individuals briefly stranded amid the surge.
Residents responded swiftly, stepping in to guide the trapped tourists away from the dangerous currents. Their coordinated effort ensured that all those present were brought to safety before the situation escalated further.
The video, shared on Instagram by one of the tourists, was posted as a cautionary account of how quickly conditions can deteriorate.

“When fun turns into a horrible situation in just two minutes. Vandri Lake,” the caption read, describing the sudden shift from a calm outing to a dangerous scenario.
The tourist also thanked a man identified as Pravin and other locals for their role in the rescue. According to the post, even those filming the incident assisted in helping stranded visitors reach safety. “No picnic or social media reel is worth risking one's life,” the message added.
Authorities and experts have consistently warned that waterfalls, streams, and rivers can become dangerous within minutes during heavy rainfall. Even when rainfall appears minimal at a tourist location, intense showers upstream can cause sudden surges in water levels.
Such conditions can trigger flash floods with little warning, while slippery surfaces, reduced visibility, and strong currents significantly heighten the risk of accidents during the monsoon season.
